It's possible in the conversation to keep them on the path of discovery that they should work with you. What's the main problem you're trying to solve, right? And so you need to clarify why they came in. There's going to be even little objections that happen here where people are like, well, I just want to get more information. I just want to check it out. Like, no, but I mean, I don't sign up for problem when we're trying to solve. And then you can dig in a little bit deeper so that they present a problem. You go to L, which is you're going to label them with a problem. And so you're basically going to summarize and restate the main reason why they came in. Okay, so the main reason you're with this, and you've gained, you know, 50 pounds in the last two years, and you're trying to lose weight. Does that sign up all right? Yes. Okay, cool. So you won't be upset with me if I help you lose the weight. C to L to now. Oh, so like, okay, so you're trying to lose this weight. Give me some examples. Like what have you done? And so you want to be thinking about while you're going through the pain cycle is total time cost, total emotional cost, and total different types of things to the pains so that you can use them in your pitch to solidify what you're going to be telling them. And one of the key questions to ask here is why do you very much flip the conversation to, well, I won't dig too much into this, but that's, that's over in the past pain and we'll get into the pain cycle during the sales training. S is selling the vacation, which is where you're going to go through the three pillar pitch, which is fitness, nutrition, accountability, and explain it very, very quickly. We're coming to a specific obstacle not to delineate every single thing that's going to be in the program, because what happens is when you do that, they one have way more things to say no to, but two, it reminds them of how much work is about to happen. And it's a lot easier to get someone to work once they've given you their money than before. We overview the past pains of why they failed to achieve that in the past. And then after that, we say, you know what, I understand what you've done. Tell them the vacation fitness, nutrition, accountability. After you sell the vacation obstacle overcomes. So that's where it's like, well, after I talked to my husband, I have to think about it. I don't make split decisions. I don't have that kind of money, blah, blah, blah, blah. All of those things, all those I'll make a decision and you're helping them get out of their own way and really explaining the way that they're trying to help them make the decision that they're having difficulty making for themselves. And then ours reinforce the sale. So even after you close the sale, you blow them away and make them feel like they really made the right decision. Because the first thing that happens if you don't do sale, make sure they understand that they're in good hands. Make sure that they know that you're going to fulfill your promise. And then ultimately strengthen the sale, strengthen their conviction so that they get better results.
